signals.headline: Kevin Warsh Nominated as Fed Chair Amid Surging US Inflation and Rate Hike Speculation | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Kevin Warsh Nominated as Fed Chair Amid Surging US Inflation and Rate Hike Speculation | YES | HIGH confidence**  
US annual core inflation has jumped to 3.9%, pushing 3-month annualized core PCE (Fed's preferred measure) to 4.56%—more than double the 2% target—pre-dating a Middle East energy shock with oil above $100/barrel.[1] President Trump's nomination of Kevin Warsh as the new Fed chair (to

signals.headline: South Korean Won Weakens on Warsh Nomination, Fed Uncertainty, and Failed US Trade Talks | YES | MED confidence**
signals.raw:
South Korean Won Weakens on Warsh Nomination, Fed Uncertainty, and Failed US Trade Talks | YES | MED confidence**  
The won slipped to 1,457/USD amid Trump's Warsh nomination raising dollar strength and inflation hawkishness concerns, compounded by stalled US-Korea trade talks where Trump threatened 25% tariffs on Korean autos and goods with no breakthrough.[2] South Korea's January exports surged
